title: eriophyllum lanatum var. hallii
intro: Eriophyllum lanatum var. hallii, also known as Hall's woolly sunflower or Hall's fleabane, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to their ability to soothe and calm the body. Eriophyllum lanatum var. hallii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
